current.task: HenryDB: profile TransactionalDB overhead - identify which MVCC operations cost most
current.summary: ROOT CAUSE FOUND: TransactionalDB routes SELECT through auto-commit DML path (begin+execute+WAL+commit+physicalize). Parse=0.009ms, MVCC=0.002ms, Inner DB=0.3ms — the 18.6ms overhead is WAL writes + physicalize for reads that don't need it. Fix: add read-only fast path for SELECT (snapshot read only, no WAL, no begin/commit). This would make SELECT 60x faster.

blocks.task: Profile HenryDB: which queries are slowest? Where are the bottlenecks?
blocks.summary: Profiled HenryDB with 5000 rows: reads fast (10-26ms), writes slow (2ms/insert with index). Full scan 4.2μs/row, GROUP BY 14.5ms, Window fn 23.9ms over 5K rows. Main bottleneck: INSERT (constraint validation + btree + heap per stmt). Optimization path: batch inserts, skip redundant constraint checks during bulk load. No query-level bottlenecks found.

queue.task: HenryDB: investigate query plan caching potential for repeated queries
queue.summary: Query plan caching investigation: Parsing is only 5.6% of total query time (0.16ms/parse vs 2.8ms/query). Execution dominates at 94.4%. Conclusion: vectorized engine optimization (speeds up data processing) is the right target, NOT prepared statements (which only skip parsing). Plan caching would save <0.2ms per query.
